tools.description: Add a comment to an existing GitHub issue, pull request, or discussion. Use this to provide feedback, answer questions, or add information to an existing conversation. For creating new items, use create_issue, create_discussion, or create_pull_request instead. IMPORTANT: Comments are subject to validation constraints enforced by the MCP server - maximum 65536 characters for the complete comment (including footer which is added automatically), 10 mentions (@username), and 50 links. Exceeding these limits will result in an immediate error with specific guidance. NOTE: By default, this tool requires discussions:write permission. If your GitHub App lacks Discussions permission, set 'discussions: false' in the workflow's safe-outputs.add-comment configuration to exclude this permission. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 1 comment(s) can be added.

body.description: The comment text in Markdown format. This is the 'body' field - do not use 'comment_body' or other variations. Provide helpful, relevant information that adds value to the conversation. CONSTRAINTS: The complete comment (your body text + automatically added footer) must not exceed 65536 characters total. Maximum 10 mentions (@username), maximum 50 links (http/https URLs). A footer (~200-500 characters) is automatically appended with workflow attribution, so leave adequate space. If these limits are exceeded, the tool call will fail with a detailed error message indicating which constraint was violated.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

item_number.description: The issue, pull request, or discussion number to comment on. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 123 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/123). If omitted, the tool auto-targets the issue, PR, or discussion that triggered this workflow. Auto-targeting only works for issue, pull_request, discussion, and comment event triggers — it does NOT work for schedule, workflow_dispatch, push, or workflow_run triggers. For those trigger types, always provide item_number explicitly, or the comment will be silently discarded.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: add_comment

tools.description: Add labels to an existing GitHub issue or pull request for categorization and filtering. Labels must already exist in the repository. For creating new issues with labels, use create_issue with the labels property instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 5 label(s) can be added.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

item_number.description: Issue or PR number to add labels to. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 456 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/456). If omitted, adds labels to the issue or PR that triggered this workflow. Only works for issue or pull_request event triggers. For schedule, workflow_dispatch, or other triggers, item_number is required — omitting it will silently skip the label operation.

labels.description: Label names to add (e.g., ['bug', 'priority-high']). Labels must exist in the repository.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: add_labels

tools.description: Report that a tool or capability needed to complete the task is not available, or share any information you deem important about missing functionality or limitations. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because the required functionality is missing or access is restricted.

alternatives.description: Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

reason.description: Explanation of why this tool is needed or what information you want to share about the limitation (max 256 characters).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tool.description: Optional: Name or description of the missing tool or capability (max 128 characters). Be specific about what functionality is needed.

tools.name: missing_tool

tools.description: Log a transparency message when no significant actions are needed. Use this to confirm workflow completion and provide visibility when analysis is complete but no changes or outputs are required (e.g., 'No issues found', 'All checks passed'). This ensures the workflow produces human-visible output even when no other actions are taken.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

message.description: Status or completion message to log. Should explain what was analyzed and the outcome (e.g., 'Code review complete - no issues found', 'Analysis complete - all tests passing').

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: noop

tools.description: Report that data or information needed to complete the task is not available. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because required data, context, or information is missing.

alternatives.description: Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).

context.description: Additional context about the missing data or where it should come from (max 256 characters).

data_type.description: Type or description of the missing data or information (max 128 characters). Be specific about what data is needed.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

reason.description: Explanation of why this data is needed to complete the task (max 256 characters).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: missing_data

annotations.title: Get a specific label from a repository.

tools.description: Get a specific label from a repository.
tools.name: get_label

tools.description: Get information about a specific issue in a GitHub repository.

method.description:
The read operation to perform on a single issue.
Options are:
1. get - Get details of a specific issue.
2. get_comments - Get issue comments.
3. get_sub_issues - Get sub-issues of the issue.
4. get_labels - Get labels assigned to the issue.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

tools.name: issue_read

tools.description: List supported issue types for repository owner (organization).

owner.description: The organization owner of the repository

tools.name: list_issue_types

tools.description: List issues in a GitHub repository. For pagination, use the 'endCursor' from the previous response's 'pageInfo' in the 'after' parameter.

after.description: Cursor for pagination. Use the endCursor from the previous page's PageInfo for GraphQL APIs.

direction.description: Order direction. If provided, the 'orderBy' also needs to be provided.

orderBy.description: Order issues by field. If provided, the 'direction' also needs to be provided.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

state.description: Filter by state, by default both open and closed issues are returned when not provided

tools.name: list_issues

tools.description: Search for issues in GitHub repositories using issues search syntax already scoped to is:issue

owner.description: Optional repository owner. If provided with repo, only issues for this repository are listed.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

query.description: Search query using GitHub issues search syntax

repo.description: Optional repository name. If provided with owner, only issues for this repository are listed.

sort.description: Sort field by number of matches of categories, defaults to best match

tools.name: search_issues

{"number":57047,"title":"[Azure.Provisioning.AppContainers] JavaComponent missing componentType discriminator and ingress property","body":"## Problem

The provisioning generator does not emit the `componentType` discriminator property or the `ingress` property for `Microsoft.App/managedEnvironments/javaComponents` resources. When comparing the generated `schema.log` against the [2025-07-01 Bicep reference](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/templates/microsoft.app/2025-07-01/managedenvironments/javacomponents?pivots=deployment-language-bicep), these properties are missing:

| Property | Bicep Type | Notes |
|----------|-----------|-------|
| `properties.componentType` | `string` | **Required** discriminator: `SpringBootAdmin` \| `SpringCloudConfig` \| `SpringCloudEureka` |
| `properties.ingress` | `object` | Applicable to SpringBootAdmin and SpringCloudEureka component types |

The `componentType` property is **required** in the Bicep reference — without it, users cannot specify which type of Java component to create.

## Root Cause

The management library (`Azure.ResourceManager.AppContainers` v1.5.0) models `JavaComponentProperties` as an abstract base class with three derived types:

- `SpringBootAdminComponent` → `ComponentType = JavaComponentType.SpringBootAdmin`
- `SpringCloudConfigComponent` → `ComponentType = JavaComponentType.SpringCloudConfig`
- `SpringCloudEurekaComponent` → `ComponentType = JavaComponentType.SpringCloudEureka`

The provisioning generator does not automatically detect this discriminator pattern for the `JavaComponent` resource's properties type.

## Proposed Fix

Update `AppContainersSpecification.cs` to add discriminator metadata for the `JavaComponentProperties` derived types using `CustomizeSimpleModel`, similar to how other specifications handle discriminators:

- [ContainerRegistrySpecification.cs](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/provisioning/Generator/src/Specifications/ContainerRegistrySpecification.cs) — uses `CustomizeSimpleModel` with `DiscriminatorName`/`DiscriminatorValue` for task steps
- [PostgreSqlSpecification.cs](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/provisioning/Generator/src/Specifications/PostgreSqlSpecification.cs) — uses `CustomizeSimpleModel` for create mode properties

Example pattern:
```csharp
CustomizeSimpleModel(m => { m.DiscriminatorName = "componentType"; m.DiscriminatorValue = "SpringBootAdmin"; });
CustomizeSimpleModel(m => { m.DiscriminatorName = "componentType"; m.DiscriminatorValue = "SpringCloudConfig"; });
CustomizeSimpleModel(m => { m.DiscriminatorName = "componentType"; m.DiscriminatorValue = "SpringCloudEureka"; });
```

The `ingress` property (on `SpringBootAdminComponent` and `SpringCloudEurekaComponent`) should also be included in the generated schema.

## Files

- Specification: `sdk/provisioning/Generator/src/Specifications/AppContainersSpecification.cs`
- Generated resource: `sdk/provisioning/Azure.Provisioning.AppContainers/src/Generated/JavaComponent.cs`
- Schema log: `sdk/provisioning/Azure.Provisioning.AppContainers/src/Generated/schema.log` (lines 1239–1268)","state":"open","html_url":"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/57047","user":{"login":"ArcturusZhang","id":10554446,"profile_url":"https://github.com/ArcturusZhang","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/10554446?v=4"},"author_association":"MEMBER","labels":["bug"],"reactions":{"total_count":1,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"hooray":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":1},"created_at":"2026-03-13T03:53:38Z","updated_at":"2026-03-13T03:53:38Z"}

{"total_count":1,"incomplete_results":false,"items":[{"id":4068665510,"number":57047,"state":"open","locked":false,"title":"[Azure.Provisioning.AppContainers] JavaComponent missing componentType discriminator and ingress property","body":"## Problem

The provisioning generator does not emit the `componentType` discriminator property or the `ingress` property for `Microsoft.App/managedEnvironments/javaComponents` resources. When comparing the generated `schema.log` against the [2025-07-01 Bicep reference](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/templates/microsoft.app/2025-07-01/managedenvironments/javacomponents?pivots=deployment-language-bicep), these properties are missing:

| Property | Bicep Type | Notes |
|----------|-----------|-------|
| `properties.componentType` | `string` | **Required** discriminator: `SpringBootAdmin` \| `SpringCloudConfig` \| `SpringCloudEureka` |
| `properties.ingress` | `object` | Applicable to SpringBootAdmin and SpringCloudEureka component types |

The `componentType` property is **required** in the Bicep reference — without it, users cannot specify which type of Java component to create.

## Root Cause

The management library (`Azure.ResourceManager.AppContainers` v1.5.0) models `JavaComponentProperties` as an abstract base class with three derived types:

- `SpringBootAdminComponent` → `ComponentType = JavaComponentType.SpringBootAdmin`
- `SpringCloudConfigComponent` → `ComponentType = JavaComponentType.SpringCloudConfig`
- `SpringCloudEurekaComponent` → `ComponentType = JavaComponentType.SpringCloudEureka`

The provisioning generator does not automatically detect this discriminator pattern for the `JavaComponent` resource's properties type.

## Proposed Fix

Update `AppContainersSpecification.cs` to add discriminator metadata for the `JavaComponentProperties` derived types using `CustomizeSimpleModel`, similar to how other specifications handle discriminators:

- [ContainerRegistrySpecification.cs](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/provisioning/Generator/src/Specifications/ContainerRegistrySpecification.cs) — uses `CustomizeSimpleModel` with `DiscriminatorName`/`DiscriminatorValue` for task steps
- [PostgreSqlSpecification.cs](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/provisioning/Generator/src/Specifications/PostgreSqlSpecification.cs) — uses `CustomizeSimpleModel` for create mode properties

Example pattern:
```csharp
CustomizeSimpleModel<SpringBootAdminComponent>(m => { m.DiscriminatorName = "componentType"; m.DiscriminatorValue = "SpringBootAdmin"; });
CustomizeSimpleModel<SpringCloudConfigComponent>(m => { m.DiscriminatorName = "componentType"; m.DiscriminatorValue = "SpringCloudConfig"; });
CustomizeSimpleModel<SpringCloudEurekaComponent>(m => { m.DiscriminatorName = "componentType"; m.DiscriminatorValue = "SpringCloudEureka"; });
```

The `ingress` property (on `SpringBootAdminComponent` and `SpringCloudEurekaComponent`) should also be included in the generated schema.

## Files

- Specification: `sdk/provisioning/Generator/src/Specifications/AppContainersSpecification.cs`
- Generated resource: `sdk/provisioning/Azure.Provisioning.AppContainers/src/Generated/JavaComponent.cs`
- Schema log: `sdk/provisioning/Azure.Provisioning.AppContainers/src/Generated/schema.log` (lines {"total_count":192,"incomplete_results":false,"items":[{"id":4055393958,"number":56930,"state":"open","locked":false,"title":"[EngSys] Build the release process for mgmt and provisioning libraries","body":"## Goal

Build a pipeline that releases a management-plane library and its corresponding provisioning library together, ensuring they always generate from the same TypeSpec commit.

## Directory Structure

```
sdk/keyvault/
  Azure.ResourceManager.KeyVault/
    tsp-location.yaml          # single source of truth for the TypeSpec commit
  Azure.Provisioning.KeyVault/
    tsp-location.yaml          # references the mgmt tsp-location.yaml via ref field
  ci.mgmt.yaml                 # CI definition covering both mgmt and provisioning packages
```

### `tsp-location.yaml` Sync Mechanism

The provisioning library's `tsp-location.yaml` should **reference the mgmt library's `tsp-location.yaml`** instead of duplicating its fields. For example:

```yaml
# Azure.Provisioning.KeyVault/tsp-location.yaml
ref: ../Azure.ResourceManager.KeyVault/tsp-location.yaml
```

The `ref` field tells the tooling to resolve `directory`, `commit`, and `repo` from the mgmt library's `tsp-location.yaml`. This ensures:

- **Single source of truth**  developers only update the mgmt `tsp-location.yaml`; the provisioning library automatically follows.
- **No manual sync**  the provisioning `tsp-location.yaml` is never directly modified, eliminating the risk of commit ID drift.
- **CI enforcement**  if the mgmt commit changes and the provisioning library is not regenerated, CI fails to alert the developer.

## Release Pipeline

![Release Pipeline Diagram](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/0771059c-aa6e-44c9-8531-263b741fda86)

### Behavior

- The mgmt and provisioning packages are released from the **same pipeline**.
- Both packages release together on every release cycle.
- If one package has no changes, there are two options to decide between:
  1. **Skip unchanged**  only release the package that has changes.
  2. **Always release both**  release both packages regardless, keeping their minor versions aligned.","author_association":"MEMBER","user":{"login":"ArcturusZhang","id":10554446,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjEwNTU0NDQ2","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/10554446?v=4","html_url":"https://github.com/ArcturusZhang","gravatar_id":"","type":"User","site_admin":false,"url":"https://api.github.com/users/ArcturusZhang","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ArcturusZhang/events{/privacy}","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ArcturusZhang/following{/other_user}","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ArcturusZhang/followers","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ArcturusZhang/gists{/gist_id}","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ArcturusZhang/orgs","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ArcturusZhang/received_events","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ArcturusZhang/repos","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ArcturusZhang/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ArcturusZhang/subscriptions"},"labels":[{"id":903972863,"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/labels/Mgmt-EngSys","name":"Mgmt-EngSys","color":"ffeb77","description":"Engineering System (Management Plane package.","default":false,"node_id":"MDU6TGFiZWwxMTc4NDQ4MTEx"},{"id":6560170331,"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/labels/Provisioning","name":"Provisioning","color":"ffeb77","description":"","default":false,"node_id":"LA_kwDOACyxMM8AAAABhwRBWw"}],"comments":1,"created_at":"2026-03-11T02:59:21Z","updated_at":"2026-03-11T03:07:55Z","url":"https://api.github.com/repos/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/56930","html_url":"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/56930","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/56930/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/56930/events","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/56930/labels{/name}","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net","reactions":{"total_count":1,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"hooray":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":1,"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/56930/reactions"},"node_id":"I_kwDOACyxMM7xuGam"},{"id":4031739607,"number":56822,"state":"open","locked":false,"title":"Provisioning Migration - SignalR","body":"## Summary

Migrate `Azure.Provisioning.SignalR` from the reflection-based provisioning generator to the new TypeSpec-based provisioning generator (`http-client-csharp-provisioning`).

## Prerequisites

- The peer mgmt library `Azure.ResourceManager.SignalR` is already using the TypeSpec-based mgmt generator (`@azure-typespec/http-client-csharp-mgmt`).

## Steps

1. Add `tsp-location.yaml` pointing to the TypeSpec spec with `emitterPackageJsonPath: "eng/azure-typespec-http-client-csharp-provisioning-emitter-package.json"`
2. Run `dotnet build /t:GenerateCode` to generate the provisioning library
3. Compare generated output against existing hand-written library
4. Identify and track any issues found
5. Address issues and verify generated output matches expected API surface

## Related

- Provisioning generator PR: #56627
- AppConfiguration migration tracker: #56747

Migration - Kubernetes","body":"## Summary

Migrate `Azure.Provisioning.Kubernetes` from the reflection-based provisioning generator to the new TypeSpec-based provisioning generator (`http-client-csharp-provisioning`).

## Prerequisites

- The peer mgmt library `Azure.ResourceManager.Kubernetes` is already using the TypeSpec-based mgmt generator (`@azure-typespec/http-client-csharp-mgmt`).

## Steps

1. Add `tsp-location.yaml` pointing to the TypeSpec spec with `emitterPackageJsonPath: "eng/azure-typespec-http-client-csharp-provisioning-emitter-package.json"`
2. Run `dotnet build /t:GenerateCode` to generate the provisioning library
3. Compare generated output against existing hand-written library
4. Identify and track any issues found
5. Address issues and verify generated output matches expected API surface

## Related

- Provisioning generator PR: #56627
- AppConfiguration migration tracker: #56747
